reasons: Baseline threads used the projectless desktop environment while candidate threads used a saved project environment.
The projectless environment has different developer instructions and link/output rules, so it is not identical except for the candidate skill.
Only one run per condition was executed for critical cases.
usable_evidence: Candidate skill discovery and actual skill/reference read trace.
Raw visible outputs, timing, token usage, tool calls, file diffs, tests, and rollout hashes.
Standalone candidate assertions and harness calibration findings.

E04.finding: The standard comparison task loaded every reference file instead of the needed research sections.

E06.finding: The run read the multi-agent protocol but still did not present roles, benefit, cost, risk, or an approval decision.

E31.finding: The ordinary local task avoided the skill body, references, and fixture governance documents.

E36.finding: Freshness research triggered, but governance was not loaded and multi-agent suitability was not assessed.

intent: Require proactive multi-agent assessment from objective task facts.
Preserve explicit user approval before starting agents.

next_run_requirements: Use a project worktree baseline identical to the candidate project except that the repository skill is disabled.
Repeat E04, E06, and E36 first against the isolated candidate patch.
Do not activate the candidate patch without user approval.
Keep rollout JSONL collection and context classification enabled.
